canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, Self-Reminder is characterized as a training-free defense method. Its operational mechanism, as described in "RED: Unleashing Token-Level Rewards from Holistic Feedback via Reward Redistribution", involves adding safety reminders both before and after a user's request. This process is applied during the inference stage, meaning it does not require any model retraining. The stated purpose of this technique is to act as a defense, as one source notes, "Self-Reminder defends by adding safety reminders before and after user requests."
papers: RED: Unleashing Token-Level Rewards from Holistic Feedback via Reward Redistribution
Refining Attention for Explainable and Noise-Robust Fact-Checking with Transformers
The Illusion of Progress: Re-evaluating Hallucination Detection inLLMs

definitions.text: Self-Reminder is a training-free defense method that adds safety reminders before and after user requests during inference.
definitions.paper: RED: Unleashing Token-Level Rewards from Holistic Feedback via Reward Redistribution
